question:
MicroStrategy announces >1000 BTC purchase November 11-17?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if MicroStrategy announces a purchase of more Bitcoin than the number specified in the title between the dates in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last date). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on announcements made within the market's designated time frame regardless of when the actual purchases were made.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MicroStrategy or Michael Saylor.

For reference, MicroStrategy's reported BTC holdings can be tracked at: https://www.strategy.com/purchases

Resolution source: https://www.strategy.com/purchases

evidences.title: Michael Saylor's Post Fuels New Bitcoin Purchase Speculation for ...
evidences.content: ... crypto background image BeInCrypto # Michael Saylor’s Post Fuels New Bitcoin Purchase Speculation for MicroStrategy Phil Haunhorst 2 min read MSTR +7.90% BTC-USD +0.56% POLA.PVT Michael Saylor, founder and executive chairman of Strategy, posted "Working ₿etter" on X on May 31, drawing immediate speculation from traders and market watchers about another Bitcoin (BTC) acquisition. The message echoes a familiar pattern that market watchers have learned to follow. Strategy has historically disclosed new Bitcoin purchases within days of similar posts, turning each update from Saylor's account into a closely followed signal for a potential 8-K filing. ## Longest Purchase Pause in Recent Months [...] ## Bitcoin Balance Sheet Scrutiny Grows A new purchase would come amid rising concern over MicroStrategy's capital position. The company spent $1.38 billion in May buying back $1.5 billion face value of its 2029 convertible notes, trimming its USD reserve to approximately $871 million from around $2 billion before the transaction. Arca chief investment officer Jeff Dorman published a direct warning about the capital flywheel, arguing that roughly $15 billion in outstanding preferred st...

evidences.title: Michael Saylor Will Never Give up on Bitcoin, His New X Post Promises — TradingView News
evidences.content: Strategy buys additional 430 BTC On Monday, Saylor addressed his millions of X followers with a tweet, announcing that Strategy had spent roughly $51.4 million to acquire an additional 430 Bitcoins. This crypto was bought at approximately $119,666 per BTC, and it propelled the company’s total holdings to 629,376 Bitcoin, valued at $72,293,274,240 at the current BTCUSD exchange rate. Strategy has acquired 430 BTC for ~$51.4 million at ~$119,666 per bitcoin and has achieved BTC Yield of 25.1% YTD 2025. As of 8/17/2025, we hodl 629,376 $BTC acquired for ~$46.15 billion at ~$73,320 per bitcoin. $MSTR $STRC $STRK $STRF $STRD [...] U.Today U.Today # Michael Saylor Will Never Give up on Bitcoin, His New X Post Promises Michael Saylor, a vocal Bitcoin evangelist and Strategy’s executive chairman, has published a new X post dedicated to BTC, as the world’s largest digital currency has fallen back to the $115,000 price level. Saylor’s message may hint that he is never going to give up on Bitcoin. This aligns with Saylor’s earlier statements that Strategy intends to always buy Bitcoin and hold “Bitcoin forever” too. Michael Saylor's Bitcoin message to community [...] Michael Saylor's Bitco...

evidences.title: MicroStrategy’s Bitcoin Strategy Nets Staggering $1.2 Billion Profit in Single Week
evidences.content: Conclusion MicroStrategy’s Bitcoin accumulation strategy has proven extraordinarily profitable, generating a $1.2 billion gain in just one week. This result stems from a disciplined, long-term approach to holding Bitcoin as a primary treasury asset. With total holdings exceeding 761,000 BTC, the company remains the dominant corporate player in the space. Its journey continues to shape perceptions of Bitcoin’s role in modern corporate finance, offering both a blueprint and a cautionary tale about volatility and conviction in the digital age. FAQs [...] In a landmark week for corporate cryptocurrency adoption, MicroStrategy’s aggressive Bitcoin accumulation strategy yielded an astonishing $1.2 billion in paper profits, as announced by executive chairman Michael Saylor on March 24, 2025. This monumental gain, equivalent to approximately 16,622 BTC, underscores the profound financial impact of the company’s unwavering commitment to Bitcoin as a primary treasury reserve asset. Consequently, this event has reignited global discussions about Bitcoin’s role in corporate finance. MicroStrategy’s Bitcoin Accumulation Strategy Explained [...] Expert Analysis on Corporate Treasury Strategy...

evidences.title: Is MicroStrategy's Bitcoin Treasury Strategy Crazy? A Comprehensive Asset Comparison
evidences.content: Dr. Oliver Hoenig ### Dr. Oliver Hoenig In recent years, MicroStrategy's decision to convert substantial portions of its treasury into Bitcoin has sparked intense debate in corporate finance circles. This analysis provides a detailed comparison of Bitcoin against traditional treasury assets through multiple lenses: risk metrics, market structure, liquidity, tax implications, and competitive dynamics. ### The Core Challenge MicroStrategy has essentially transformed from a business intelligence software company into a leveraged Bitcoin investment vehicle. This raises fundamental questions about corporate treasury management, risk exposure, and fiduciary responsibility. ### Comparative Risk Profile [...] ### Comparative Risk Profile When compared to traditional treasury assets, Bitcoin presents significantly higher volatility: Annual Volatility: 60-100% (vs. 10-20% for traditional assets) Maximum Drawdown: ~80% (vs. 15-55% for others) Daily Price Swings: 2-5% (vs. 0.3-1% for others) These metrics suggest Bitcoin introduces extraordinary risk levels for a corporate treasury asset. ### Market Structure and Liquidity The liquidity profile reveals concerning dynamics: Market Cap: Bitco...
evidences.query: MicroStrategy bitcoin accumulation analysis institutional treasury strategy analyst report

evidences.title: MicroStrategy Stock and Bitcoin 2025: Dive into ...
evidences.content: MicroStrategy will continue its aggressive acquisition strategy for Bitcoin. The company announced a “21/21” strategy to raise $42 billion. This includes at-the-market share sales and fixed-income securities to purchase more Bitcoin. Andrew Kang, the CFO, commented on their treasury strategy. Bitcoin holdings increased by 11% this quarter. They achieved a year-to-date BTC yield of 17.8%. They also cut annualized interest expenses by $24 million. This move shows the company’s commitment to integrating cryptocurrency into its corporate strategy. It may further its financial standing and market influence. For Forex retail traders, understanding MicroStrategy’s expansion goals is crucial. This helps them grasp the interplay between cryptocurrency and global currency.

evidences.title: The MicroStrategy-Style Treasury Strategy: Why It Works and How to Value It
evidences.content: The MicroStrategy Treasury Strategy in a Nutshell:MicroStrategy (now rebranded as “Strategy” Inc.) was originally a software firm, but in 2020 it pioneered the idea of a corporate Bitcoin treasury. The company invested its cash into Bitcoin and kept raising money (via stock and bond issuances) to buy more Bitcoin. Over a few years, MicroStrategy accumulated hundreds of thousands of BTC. This “Bitcoin side hustle” went stratospheric: MicroStrategy’s share price rose roughly 20-fold, catapulting its market cap to tens of billions of dollars. Effectively, MicroStrategy became a publicly traded Bitcoin fund, except that its stock began trading at a huge premium to the value of its Bitcoin holdings. By late 2024, its market cap was about double the value of the BTC on its balance sheet. In [...] Analysts estimate that a large part of MSTR’s 100%+ premium to NAV comes from “market recognition that the company will likely continue purchasing more Bitcoin over time,” as well as an embedded expectation that each Bitcoin itself will appreciate in value. In effect, the stock market is discounting the future value of a growing hoard of Bitcoin, not just the present holdings. [...] Expectati...

evidences.title: Relentless Bitcoin Accumulation: Strategy Snaps Up 1,045 More BTC — TradingView News
evidences.content: NewsBTC NewsBTC # Relentless Bitcoin Accumulation: Strategy Snaps Up 1,045 More BTC According to recent filings, MicroStrategy (rebranded to Strategy) added 1,045 Bitcoin to its holdings this week. The move pushed the firm’s average purchase price to $70,086, after buying at levels above $100,000 in recent weeks. So far, the Michael Saylor-led company has spent more than $40 billion on Bitcoin and is lining up more financing to keep the buys coming. Weekly Bitcoin Purchases Continue Strategy’s drip-feed gameplan has become routine. The firm bought just under 2,000 BTC over the last two weeks, but split those into smaller chunks. [...] That steady rhythm helps avoid big bumps in the market. It also keeps the firm in the headlines on a weekly basis. Traders watch closely. When the company buys, prices tend to move up. Strategy has acquired 1,045 BTC for ~$110.2 million at ~$105,426 per bitcoin and has achieved BTC Yield of 17.1% YTD 2025. As of 6/8/2025, we hodl 582,000 $BTC acquired for ~$40.79 billion at ~$70,086 per bitcoin. $MSTR $STRK $STRF $STRD Rather than issue new MSTR shares, Strategy tapped its STRK and STRF stocks. The latest filings show $62.7 million in STRK and $43....

evidences.title: Deconstructing Strategy (MSTR): Premium, Leverage, and Capital ...
evidences.content: ## Funding the Bitcoin Treasury Strategy In October 2024, Strategy announced the ambitious 21/21 capital plan to buy BTC with $42B raised by selling $21B of MSTR equity and $21B of fixed income securities by 2027. In the original plan, Strategy would sell $5B of equity in 2025, $7B in 2026, and $9B in 2027. Meanwhile, it would sell fixed income securities at a similar pace of $5B, $7B, and $9B in 2025, 2026, and 2027. These debt issuances targeted a leverage ratio between 20-30%. Strategy leader Michael Saylor calls this “intelligent leverage,” not for speculation, but to strategically acquire a “dominant digital asset.” [...] From a strategic standpoint, Strategy offers several key lessons for firms considering implementing a Bitcoin treasury strategy. First, there must be a clearly defined objective behind adopting a Bitcoin treasury. Strategy exemplifies this through its singular focus on increasing BTC holdings per share, rather than adhering to traditional financial metrics such as dilution or enterprise value in fiat terms. Under Michael Saylor’s leadership, the firm has reoriented its valuation framework around Bitcoin itself. Saylor views success not in terms of dollar-b...

evidences.title: What Is Strategy (MSTR)? The Bitcoin Treasury Company
evidences.content: MicroStrategy Is Raising $584 Million to Stack Even More Bitcoin Throughout the year, the firm would launch additional preferred stock offerings to help fund its Bitcoin purchases, granting different types of investors access to different risk-adjusted products like Stretch (STRC), Stride (STRD), Strife (STRF), and Stream (STRE) in addition to Strike. The strategy of raising debt to buy Bitcoin has been adopted by other publicly traded companies like MARA and Riot Platforms. In December, Saylor likened this strategy to the development of Manhattan real estate, telling CNBC that, "Every time Manhattan real estate goes up in value, they issue more debt to develop more real estate." [...] The firm signaled in October 2024 that it has no plans on slowing down in the future. At that time, Strategy announced plans to raise up to $42 billion in order to add more of the leading cryptocurrency asset to its balance sheet. Seeking more though, the firm passed a vote in January 2025 to implement a 30x increase of its Class A common shares, allowing it to add even more Bitcoin than initially planned. Days later, the company announced the offering of a new stock, Strike (STRK)–as a new way fo...

evidences.title: MSTR Investor Relations - Microstrategy Inc
evidences.content: Saylor attended the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) on an Air Force ROTC scholarship, where he earned a degree in aeronautics and astronautics along with science, technology, and society. In 1989, he co-founded MicroStrategy with Sanju Bansal, initially focusing on providing data analysis software for businesses. Under his leadership, MicroStrategy became a prominent player in the business intelligence industry. In addition to his work with MicroStrategy, Saylor is known for his advocacy of Bitcoin. In 2020, he gained significant attention by shifting MicroStrategy's corporate strategy toward acquiring and holding Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset. His decisions and public endorsements of cryptocurrency have made him a prominent figure in the digital asset space. [...] Shirish Jajodia Senior Director of Treasury & Head of Investor Relations Shirish Jajodia is an accomplished executive known for his role as a Senior Vice President of Operations at MicroStrategy Inc., a leading worldwide provider of enterprise analytics and mobility software. At MicroStrategy, Jajodia plays a crucial role in overseeing and optimizing various aspects of the company's operations. His r...

evidences.title: Strategy Announces Proposed STRE Perpetual Preferred Stock Offering
evidences.content: Strategy intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including the acquisition of bitcoin and for working capital. [...] Strategy Inc (Nasdaq: STRF/STRC/STRK/STRD/MSTR) is the world’s first and largest Bitcoin Treasury Company. We are a publicly traded company that has adopted Bitcoin as our primary treasury reserve asset. By using proceeds from equity and debt financings, as well as cash flows from our operations, we strategically accumulate Bitcoin and advocate for its role as digital capital. Our treasury strategy is designed to provide investors varying degrees of economic exposure to Bitcoin by offering a range of securities, including equity and fixed-income instruments. In addition, we provide industry-leading AI-powered enterprise analytics software, advancing our vision of Intelligence Everywhere. We leverage our development capabilities to explore innovation in Bitcoin applications, integrating [...] to explore innovation in Bitcoin applications, integrating analytics expertise with our commitment to digital asset growth. We believe our combination of operational excellence, strategic Bitcoin reserve, and focus on technological inn...
